content: In Puranic literature, rivers, lakes, and water conservation hold significant importance as they are considered holy and are believed to have the power to fulfill desires and grant salvation. According to the Brahma Purana [1], taking a bath in these holy waters, performing Japa, Homa, and Pitrtarpana can fulfill all cherished desires of men and grant enjoyment and salvation. The text mentions twenty-five divine rivers, including the Ganga, which are considered sacred and are worshipped by devotees. Similarly, in the Mahabharata [2], various sacred rivers such as the Bhagirathi, Kalindi, and Saraswati are mentioned, which are personified and worship the Lord Varuna. These references highlight the importance of preserving and respecting these water bodies.
